title: DOM Introduction
content:
### What is the DOM?

The **DOM (Document Object Model)** is a programming interface for HTML documents. It represents the page as a **tree of objects** that can be modified.

---

### The DOM Tree

When the browser loads an HTML page, it creates a tree structure:

```
Document
 └── html
      ├── head
      │    ├── title
      │    └── meta
      └── body
           ├── h1
           ├── p
           └── div
                ├── span
                └── img
```

---

### What Can You Do with the DOM?

- **Change** HTML elements and attributes
- **Add/remove** elements dynamically
- **Respond** to user events (clicks, typing, etc.)
- **Update** CSS styles
- **Animate** elements on the page

---

### The `document` Object

The `document` object is the **entry point** to the DOM. It represents the entire HTML page.

```js
console.log(document.title);     // Page title
console.log(document.URL);       // Page URL
console.log(document.body);      // Body element
```

---

> **Key Insight:** The DOM is not JavaScript -- it is a browser API. JavaScript is just the language used to interact with it. Other languages (like Python) can also manipulate the DOM.
